User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Atelier Insights application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

googleId.description: User's Google ID, if they signed up with Google.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a service offered by the Atelier, such as tailoring adjustments, dressmaking, or customizations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the service entity.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents an expense incurred by the Atelier, categorized by type (materials, rent, supplies).

id.description: Unique identifier for the expense entity.

date.description: Date when the expense was incurred.

ExpenseCategory.title: ExpenseCategory
ExpenseCategory.description: Represents a category of expenses, such as materials, rent, or supplies.

id.description: Unique identifier for the expense category entity.

description.description: Detailed description of the expense category.

Revenue.title: Revenue
Revenue.description: Represents revenue generated by the Atelier from various services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the revenue entity.

date.description: Date when the revenue was generated.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project undertaken by the Atelier, linking services, expenses, and revenue.

id.description: Unique identifier for the project entity.

serviceIds.description: References to Services. (Relationship: Project N:N Service)

expenseIds.description: References to Expenses. (Relationship: Project N:N Expense)

revenueIds.description: References to Revenues. (Relationship: Project N:N Revenue)

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores services offered by the Atelier. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their services.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the service.

definition.description: Stores expenses incurred by the Atelier. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their expenses.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the expense.

definition.description: Stores expense categories. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their expense categories.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: expenseCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the expense category.

definition.description: Stores revenue generated by the Atelier. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their revenues.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: revenueId
params.description: The unique identifier of the revenue.

definition.description: Stores projects undertaken by the Atelier. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their projects.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to securely manage Atelier Insights data, focusing on user-owned data and supporting efficient queries. User data is stored under `/users/{userId}`, enabling path-based authorization. Services, Expenses, Expense Categories, Revenues, and Projects are stored as subcollections under the user, maintaining the user's ownership and facilitating secure data access. This structure prioritizes authorization independence by avoiding `get()` calls in security rules, thanks to the path-based ownership model. This also supports the QAPs, particularly secure list operations, by ensuring that users can only access data under their own user ID.
